## Houston 0.4.7

A reliability fix for users whose machines had a different copy of Claude Code already installed. Sessions could fail immediately on the first message with a vague "app error" toast. This release makes Houston use its own pinned Claude Code copy directly, so an older or unrelated `claude` install on the same machine no longer interferes.

### What's fixed

- **Sessions no longer die at the first message** when an unrelated copy of Claude Code lives elsewhere on your computer. Houston now talks to the version it manages itself, which is always kept in sync with what Houston was tested against.
- **App errors get better labels in our diagnostics.** When a session does fail, the underlying cause is now bucketed correctly instead of collapsing into a generic "unknown" tag, which makes it possible to spot regressions like this faster next time.

### Before you upgrade

- **Mac:** quit Houston before installing. macOS does not always replace a running app cleanly. If in doubt, remove `/Applications/Houston.app` and drag the new copy in.
- **Windows:** the auto-updater handles this for you. If you're installing fresh, expect one SmartScreen "Run anyway" click on first install. SignPath code-signing is still in flight.

### If you are on 0.4.4 or later (Mac)

Houston should detect 0.4.7, download it, install it, and relaunch into the new version on its own.

### If you are on 0.4.2 or earlier (Mac)

Auto-update will not pull you forward from those builds. Quit Houston, drag a fresh DMG over `/Applications/Houston.app`, and reopen. After that one manual step you are back on the healthy updater path and future versions apply automatically.

### Known limitations

- **Windows ARM64 not yet shipped.** Windows is x64-only. ARM64 is on the roadmap once we have demand to justify the second build pipeline.
- **Windows MSI not yet OS-code-signed.** SignPath Foundation integration is in flight. Until then, expect one SmartScreen "Run anyway" click on first install only.
- **Mobile companion is still beta.** Expect occasional reconnect hiccups. Keep your Mac awake while using it.
